hero.title: Microsurgical Root-End Surgery (Apicoectomy)
hero.subtitle: Saving your natural tooth, even after a failed root canal

intro.paragraph: Sometimes, even after a root canal, infection can persist. That's where microsurgical root-end surgery, also known as an apicoectomy, becomes a game-changer. This minimally invasive surgical procedure involves removing the infected tip of the tooth's root and sealing it, preserving your natural tooth and avoiding extraction.

sections.title: Why Is It Needed?
sections.intro: A previously treated root canal may become reinfected due to:
bullets: Undetected accessory canals branching from the main canal
Calcification (narrowing or hardening) of the canals over time
sections.outro: When traditional root canal retreatment isn't possible or effective, an endodontic surgical approach is often the best solution.

sections.title: What Happens During the Procedure?
sections.intro: Using local anesthesia, we gently numb the area and make a small incision in the gum to access the tip of the affected root. With the help of a dental operating microscope, we:
bullets: Trim a few millimeters of the root tip
Place a biocompatible filling to seal the root canal
Close the gum with fine sutures for optimal healing
sections.outro: The entire procedure is precise, quick, and comfortable, with minimal pain, faster healing, and long-term success.

sections.title: Advanced Technology for Advanced Care
sections.intro: At Prudentia, we combine expert skill with state-of-the-art dental technology to ensure the best outcomes:
bullets: Ultrasonic Instruments that gently clean infected tissue using high-frequency vibration
sections.outro: These tools allow us to target only the affected area while preserving healthy surrounding tissues.

sections.title: The Outcome?
sections.intro: A successful apicoectomy can restore your tooth, gum, and bone to full health, often lasting many years without further issues. It's a conservative, effective solution when retreatment isn't an option but tooth preservation still is.

conclusion.title: Don't Extract, Save Your Tooth
conclusion.paragraph: Explore the benefits of microsurgical endodontics today. Book a consultation with Dr. Bhushan at Prudentia Micro Dental Care, Pimple Saudagar and take the first step toward lasting oral health.

items.question: How long does an apicoectomy procedure take?
items.answer: Most apicoectomy procedures take between 30-90 minutes, depending on the complexity of the root structure and location of the tooth.

items.question: Is the procedure painful?
items.answer: We use effective local anesthesia to ensure you feel no pain during the procedure. After the anesthesia wears off, most patients experience only mild discomfort that can be managed with over-the-counter pain medication.

items.question: What is the recovery time?
items.answer: Most patients can return to normal activities the day after surgery. Complete healing of the surgical site typically takes 1-2 weeks, during which we recommend avoiding hard foods on the treated side.

items.question: What is the success rate of an apicoectomy?
items.answer: Modern microsurgical apicoectomy procedures have a success rate of approximately 85-90% when performed by a specialist using advanced technology and techniques.

finalCta.title: Ready to Save Your Natural Tooth?
finalCta.paragraph: Contact Prudentia Micro Dental Care today to schedule your consultation and learn if microsurgical root-end treatment is right for you.
